breakClass: left-join null member access / non-nullable value-column read on a possibly-null DefaultIfEmpty() entity in a server-side projection (EF Core 10 shaper reads SQL NULL into a non-nullable CLR value type â€” decimal/int/DateTime/enum â€” and throws; EF6 silently produced default(T))
why: Restaurant-POS create summary; identical SEPayments left-join 500. Legacy identical.
finalFix:
Mirror legacy EF6's default(decimal)=0 behavior by null-guarding the DefaultIfEmpty `d` entity in the projection (POSRESController.cs lines 901-902):

  SEPaidAmount = d != null ? d.SEPaidAmount : 0,
  SEDueAmount = a.SEGrandTotal - (d != null ? d.SEPaidAmount : 0),

This is output-preserving: when `d` matches it yields the same value as today; when `d` is the null side it yields 0 (exactly what EF6 produced) instead of throwing. The ternary translates server-side to `CASE WHEN d.SEPaidAmount IS NOT NULL ...` / COALESCE and avoids the non-nullable-decimal NULL read. (Equivalent alternative: make the reads nullable with `(decimal?)d.SEPaidAmount ?? 0`.) The hunter's proposed fix is exactly this and is correct.
reasoning:
I could NOT refute this; independent inspection confirms it is a genuine EF Core 10 port-break of the flagged class.

LOCATION & CODE: QuickNetCore/Controllers/POSRESController.cs, method Create (the [HttpPost] POS-RES create), lines 878-911. The query is reached in the print branch (action == "print"/"print_order", line 808) in the live `else` block (line 875) â€” the sibling `if (1==2)` block at line 811 is dead code, so 878-911 is the executed path.

(a) SERVER-SIDE: CONFIRMED. The query is `(from a in db.SalesEntrys join ... join d in db.SEPayments ... into pay from d in pay.DefaultIfEmpty() ... where a.SalesEntryId == salesEntryId select new {...}).FirstOrDefault()`. It is a composed IQueryable enumerated by `.FirstOrDefault()` with NO prior `.ToList()`/`.AsEnumerable()`/`.AsAsyncEnumerable()` boundary and is not a group-aggregate over an IGrouping. The projection translates to SQL and the shaper materializes on the server side. `d` is the LEFT (null) side of a `DefaultIfEmpty()` left join.

(b) WOULD 500: CONFIRMED on edge data. Entity SEPayment (QuickNetCore/Models/SalesEntry.cs:404) declares `public decimal SEPaidAmount { get; set; }` â€” a NON-NULLABLE decimal. The projection reads `SEPaidAmount = d.SEPaidAmount` (line 901) and `SEDueAmount = a.SEGrandTotal - d.SEPaidAmount` (line 902) with NO null guard. When the `d` left-join produces the null side, EF Core 10's shaper reads SQL NULL into a non-nullable CLR decimal and throws (InvalidOperationException â€” "Nullable object must have a value"/cast), returning a 500. EF6 silently produced default(decimal)=0, so legacy did not throw. `a` (SalesEntrys) is the driving table filtered by `a.SalesEntryId == salesEntryId` and is never null, so only `d` is at risk â€” and the unguarded non-nullable read on the null side is exactly the flagged failure mode.

PORT REGRESSION: CONFIRMED IDENTICAL. _archive/legacy-mvc5/Controllers/POSRESController.cs lines 875-908 are byte-for-byte the same query (same DefaultIfEmpty left join on SEPayments, same `SEPaidAmount = d.SEPaidAmount`, same `SEDueAmount = a.SEGrandTotal - d.SEPaidAmount`). It worked under EF6, breaks under EF Core 10 â€” a true port regression.

SEVERITY NUANCE (refines hunter's implied 'high' to MED): In the normal Create flow, lines 722-745 UNCONDITIONALLY insert one SEPayment row for this salesEntryId (db.SEPayments.Add(SEpay); db.SaveChanges()) before the print query runs, so on the happy path `d` matches and is non-null. The null side is only reached on edge data: an orphaned/missing SEPayments row (partial-save failure, concurrent delete, or legacy data without a payment row). It is reachable but not the common path â€” hence med, not high. It still 500s when hit, so verdict = confirmed. The hunter's class, claim, and proposed fix are all correct; only severity should be med.
